chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – Wearing the silk and the brocade

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/335)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/335)
text: سَأَلَهُ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ عَنِ الْخَمِيصَةِ وَ أَنَا عِنْدَهُ سَدَاهَا الْإِبْرِيسَمُ أَ يَلْبَسُهَا وَ كَانَ وَجَدَ الْبَرْدَ فَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَلْبَسَهَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Salih Bin Al Sandy, from Ja’far Bin Basheer, from Abu Al Hassan Al Ahmasy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Abu Saeed asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the gown, and I was in his<sup>asws</sup> presence, its hemming being of silk, can one wear it and he was feeling cold?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> instructed him that he can wear it’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Salih ibn al-Sindy from Ja‘far ibn Bashir from abu al-Hassan al-Ahmasiy who has said the following: “Abu Sa‘id once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about al-Khamisah (a square-shaped gown with prints), when I was there, with its warp of abrisam (silk from silk worm); if it can be used in very cold weather. He (the Imam) commanded him to wear it.’”
